svg.text: LSF — LANGUE DES SIGNES FRANCAISE
GRAMMAR — SPATIAL SYNTAX
LSF WORD ORDER
LSF: tends toward SVO with frequent topic-comment structures
HOMME [topic] LIVRE DONNER: "The man, [he] gives a book"
Flexible: spatial grammar reduces need for rigid word order
LSF VERB AGREEMENT
Directional verbs: move between loci to encode subject and object
Non-directional verbs: subject/object shown by loci + separate agreement signs
Same agreement system as ASL (inherited from LSF parent)
LSF ASPECT SYSTEM
Aspect encoded in movement: repeated = habitual • fast = sudden
No morphological tense on verbs; time signed separately or from context
Future timeline: space in front of signer • past: behind signer
LSF CLASSIFIERS
Proforms (classifiers): same system as ASL (inherited)
Handling classifiers, size-and-shape specifiers, semantic classifiers
DGS GRAMMAR · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
DGS WORD ORDER: SOV
DGS: SOV word order (Subject-Object-Verb)
MANN BUCH GEBEN-SOV: "The man gives the book" (SOV)
Mirrors German spoken grammar: German verb moves to end in subordinate
DGS VS LSF GRAMMAR
LSF: SVO-preferred • DGS: SOV-preferred (notable difference!)
Both: topic-comment • both: spatial verb agreement
DGS SOV: possibly influenced by German spoken word order
GRAMMAR COMPARISON
Both: spatial grammar, NMM question-marking, verb agreement
Both: aspect over tense • both: topic-comment structures
Key difference: SVO (LSF) vs SOV (DGS) base word order
Sign language grammar partially shaped by surrounding spoken language
PLATE 64c ALT · LSF / DGS · GRAMMAR
